prompt: high-end hyper-realistic wedding fashion portrait, elegant European interior, cinematic 16:9 widescreen. The bride is positioned near the center-left, framed from mid-thigh to the top of her head. A long cathedral veil extends widely across the right half of the frame—creating a luminous, translucent visual balance with the soft glowing window on the left. Subject: young adult bride, slender and elegant figure, fair and softly glowing skin. Exquisite three-quarter profile—torso turned away toward the right of the lens, head gently looking back from the shoulder directly into the lens. Shoulders relaxed and slightly tilted, creating an elegant S-curve at the neck, waist, and hips. Head slightly tilted toward the raised shoulder. Calm, warm, understated romantic expression—a faint closed-mouth smile, soft and focused gaze. Intimate, dignified, and serene atmosphere; not exaggerated or overly commercial. Hairstyle: deep rich coffee-brown long hair, loose natural waves falling behind the shoulders and extending down the back. Silky texture, a few delicate flyaway strands illuminated by window light. Delicate, thinly separated fringe on the forehead, with longer strands framing the temples and cheeks. Sophisticated and soft, without a stiff or heavily styled appearance. The wedding veil is secured at the back crown of the head, partially covered by hair. Face and Makeup: refined and balanced features—soft arched eyebrows, almond-shaped brown eyes, slender bridge of the nose, gentle rounded cheeks, natural rose-beige lip color. Exquisite bridal makeup: glowing natural skin, pale peach blush, softly defined lashes, fine understated eyeliner, subtle taupe eye shadow, light glossy pink-nude lips, restrained highlights on cheekbones and nose bridge. Real pores, subtle tonal variations. No plastic-looking skin or excessive beautification. Gown: elegant ivory white strapless wedding dress. Top: clean structural corset-style bodice—smooth satin or Mikado silk texture, gently sculpted sweetheart neckline, delicate internal boning, fitted to the waist. Minimalist, modern, and architectural. Below the waist: pale ivory tulle and exquisite lace in a soft A-line skirt—delicate embroidered floral and vine patterns especially over the hips and upper thighs, lace gradually blending into the fabric. Embroidery is delicate, tone-on-tone, intricate rather than heavy or shiny. Veil: long transparent cathedral ivory tulle veil, starting from the back of the head, draped diagonally across the right side of the frame, passing behind the shoulder and extending to the bottom edge. Multi-layered translucent texture—visible fine mesh, delicate folds, exquisite layered edges, soft glowing edges through the sunlight. Broad triangular shape as a major compositional element. Light and airy, not opaque or stiff. Jewelry: delicate silver or white gold bridal earrings—small floral crystal clusters with single glowing pearl drops. A narrow polished gold bracelet on the wrist of the lower arm. A delicate small gemstone ring on one finger. All jewelry is refined, small, with realistic reflections. No necklace—bare neckline and shoulders kept clean and elegant. Pose: near-side arm naturally hanging by the side—elbow slightly bent, relaxed fingers near the outer hip and upper thigh. Hands clearly visible, anatomically accurate proportions, natural knuckles, separated fingers, ring visible. Far-side arm mostly hidden behind the torso. Chest slightly lifted, waistline softly defined. Scene: luxury bridal salon, wedding venue, or classic apartment interior. Far-left of the frame: tall floor-to-ceiling French window with cream-colored slender panes—strong diffused daylight entering, forming a bright vertical band of light at the frame's edge. Pale beige curtains hung in long folds beside the window. Background wall: traditional European wainscot molding, tall rectangular recessed panels, restrained classical decorative lines, in tones of warm ivory, champagne beige, and light taupe. Environment softly blurred. Bottom-left background: blurred cream-white flowers and low-key greenery (roses, hydrangeas, or peonies), placed low near the floor or on a small table. Top-right background: faint floral arrangements blurred into light organic shapes. Lighting: warm diffused natural window light from the left side, slightly behind the bride. Brightest illumination along the crown, facial edges, bare shoulders, and layers of the veil. Soft highlights on the satin bodice, gentle shadow shaping on the far side of the face and torso. The veil catches and scatters light forming a brilliant ivory aura without losing texture. Subtle warm reflections from the beige room soften the shadows. Tones: nearly monochromatic—ivory, cream, champagne, warm beige, light nude pink, soft taupe, pearl white, dark chocolate brown hair. Restrained saturation, warm highlights, creamy skin tones, slightly understated shadows. No pure blue-white fabrics, cold grey skin, or strong yellow/orange tones. Composition: tall window forms a glowing vertical line on the left. The bride's face near the upper-left third intersection. The veil flows into the open space on the right. Eyes are the sharpest focus, followed by eyelashes, lips, earrings, hair strands, bodice edges, and nearby veil texture. Distant wall panels, flowers, and window details dissolve into creamy bokeh. Camera and Style: high-end Korean bridal photography, luxury wedding editorial, fine art fashion photography. Full-frame 85mm or 105mm, f/1.8–f/2.5, low ISO, soft natural exposure. Gentle lens compression, extremely smooth background separation, delicate halos at bright highlights, realistic fabric rendering, fine cinematic grain. High dynamic range: sunlight hitting the veil preserves mesh detail, while shadows in the hair maintain dimensionality.
